zh.Lyme Regis, West Dorset, Dorsetshire, En: 英國多瑟特萊 雷吉
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Coker於1500年出生在英國多瑟特萊 雷吉時，她的父親John Coker II20歲，母親Eleanor Deleveares16歲。她於大約1520年與Robert Jourdaine在英國英格蘭多瑟特梅爾可姆 雷吉結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1564年在她的家鄉享年64歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭多瑟特維姆斯梅爾可姆 雷吉 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Coker于1500年出生在英国多瑟特萊 雷吉时，她的父亲John Coker II20岁，母亲Eleanor Deleveares16岁。她于大约1520年与Robert Jourdaine在英国英格兰多瑟特梅爾可姆 雷吉结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1564年在她的家乡享年64岁，埋葬在英国英格兰多瑟特維姆斯梅爾可姆 雷吉 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Coker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from a group of villages in Somerset named with Coker, from a Celtic river name meaning ‘crooked’.

variants.name: Cocker
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): nickname for a quarrelsome person, from Middle English coker ‘fighter, wrangler’.
English: occupational name, ‘one who puts up hay in cocks’, a seasonal occupation.
Probably an Americanized form of German   Kocher  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Coker was born in 1500, in Lyme Regis, Dorset, England, her father, John Coker II, was 20 and her mother, Eleanor Deleveares, was 16. She married Robert Jourdaine about 1520, in Melcombe Regis, Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1564, in her hometown, at the age of 64, and was buried in Melcombe Regis Cemetery, Weymouth, Dorset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Coker est née en 1500, à Lyme Regis, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, John Coker II, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Deleveares, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Jourdaine environ 1520, à Melcombe Regis, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1564, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Melcombe Regis Cemetery, Weymouth, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Lei sposato Robert Jourdaine circa 1520, nel Melcombe Regis, Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 3 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Jane Cokerが1500年にイギリスドールセトリメ レジスで生まれた時，父John Coker IIは20歳，母Eleanor Delevearesは16歳であった。約1520年にイギリスイギリスドールセトメルコンベ レジスでRobert Jourdaineと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1564年に故郷で64歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスドールセトウエイモウスメルコンベ レジス セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Melcombe Regis Cemetery, Melcombe Regis,: 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 베이모우스 멜코브 레지스 세멧리
ko.lifeSummary: Jane Coker(이)가 1500년 영국 도르세트 림 레지스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Coker II(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor Deleveares(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 대략 1520년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 멜코브 레지스에서 Robert Jourdaine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1564년 그녀의 고향에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 베이모우스 멜코브 레지스 세멧리에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Robert Jourdaine aproximadamente 1520, em Melcombe Regis, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.Melcombe Regis Cemetery, Melcombe Regis,: мелкомб регис кеметири, вимоут, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Coker родилась в 1500 г. в местности лим регис, дорсет, Англия, её отцу по имени John Coker II было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Deleveares, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Jourdaine около 1520 г. в местности мелкомб регис, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1564 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности мелкомб регис кеметири, вимоут, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Coker nació en 1500, en Lyme Regis, Dorset, Inglaterra, su papá, John Coker II, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Eleanor Deleveares, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Robert Jourdaine aproximadamente 1520, en Melcombe Regis, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1564, en su pueblo, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Melcombe Regis Cemetery, Weymouth, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
